leads.name: Notgrass History
leads.notes: Family-run homeschool history curriculum publisher since 1999. Ray and Charlene Notgrass produce grade 1-12 history, government, and economics curriculum with student texts, teacher guides, and activity books. Clear short-run manufacturing need for multi-component curriculum sets. Email extrapolated from common name format; verify before sending.

leads.source: Publisher research / Tabletop Academy Press website
leads.name: Tabletop Academy Press (Denise Gaskins)
leads.notes: Veteran math educator and author publishing playful math activity books through her own press. Math You Can Play series and other titles. Email confirmed via search. Solo author-publisher with established homeschool audience and clear ongoing print need.

leads.name: Well-Trained Mind Press (Susan Wise Bauer)
leads.notes: Publisher of widely-used classical homeschool curricula including Story of the World, Writing With Ease, and First Language Lessons. Email confirmed via search. Small family press with 5-8 new titles per year, large and actively-used backlist, and a growing classical homeschool market.

leads.name: Redleaf Press (Nikki Darling-Kuria)
leads.notes: Leading nonprofit publisher for early childhood professionals, division of Think Small in St. Paul, MN. Midwest proximity is a natural angle. 8-12 titles per year. Director Nikki Darling-Kuria confirmed via ZoomInfo/LinkedIn. Email extrapolated from domain; verify before sending.

leads.name: Brave Writer (Julie Bogart)
leads.notes: 26-year-old homeschool writing curriculum program with 17,000+ community members. Julie Bogart publishes physical curriculum handbooks (Dart, Arrow, Boomerang, Help for High School). Email confirmed via search. Cincinnati, OH location puts her under three hours from Ann Arbor -- regional proximity angle.

leads.name: Gather 'Round Homeschool (Rebecca Spooner)
leads.notes: Fast-growing Christian homeschool unit study curriculum publisher. Rebecca Spooner built from solo operation to team-based publishing with annual catalog expansion. Generic support email is the primary business contact. Multiple new units published per year.

leads.name: Build Your Library (Emily Cook)
leads.notes: Literature-based secular homeschool curriculum for K-12. Creator Emily Cook publishes physical curriculum guides for an underserved market segment. Email extrapolated from site; contact form also available at buildyourlibrary.com/contact-us/

leads.name: Due Season Press (Angela Watson)
leads.notes: National Board Certified Teacher turned author-publisher who founded Due Season Press. Has published 6 books reaching 8 million educators annually via Truth for Teachers podcast and website. Clear established publishing track record with growing catalog. Email extrapolated from site domain.

leads.name: Bright Ring Publishing (MaryAnn Kohl)
leads.notes: Publisher of award-winning children's art activity books since 1985. MaryAnn Kohl located near Bellingham, WA. info@brightring.com SMTP-verified as dead (550 mailbox does not exist, June 30 2026). No direct email address found -- website has contact form only. Draft outreach not possible until verified email is found. Some titles published through larger houses (Chicago Review Press, Gryphon House) but Bright Ring continues independent publishing for direct sales.

leads.name: Learning Sciences International (Michael Toth)
leads.notes: Publisher of teacher professional development resources built around the Marzano framework. West Palm Beach, FL. Books go directly into schools and district training programs. Original domain learningsciences.com redirects to instructionalempowerment.com (Outlook/Microsoft MX). SMTP probe blocked by Spamhaus PBL on sandbox IP -- not a dead mailbox, a probe block. Address sourced from updated domain. Founder/CEO Michael Toth confirmed via search.

leads.name: Sibylline Press (Julia Park Tracey)
leads.notes: Independent literary press focused on women authors over 50. Grass Valley, CA. Julia Park Tracey is Acquisitions Editor -- email confirmed via search. 12-15 titles per year. Strong editorial vision and growing catalog in an underserved publishing niche.

leads.name: Moon Tide Press (Eric Morago)
leads.notes: Independent poetry press in Whittier, CA. Eric Morago took over as publisher in 2017 and has continued publishing strong voices in contemporary poetry. Email confirmed via search. 6-10 titles per year at short print runs -- ideal manufacturing profile.

leads.name: Fertility Food Publishing (Lily Nichols)
leads.notes: Registered dietitian Lily Nichols self-publishes her evidence-based prenatal nutrition books (Real Food for Pregnancy, Real Food for Fertility) through Fertility Food Publishing. Strong established readership, multiple reprints likely. Email SMTP-verified 250 OK (June 30 2026). High-quality self-publisher with clear ongoing print volume.

leads.name: Parallax Press (Rachel Neumann)
leads.notes: Berkeley, CA-based publisher of mindfulness and contemplative literature since 1986. Publisher Rachel Neumann leads an active publishing program. 500-5,000 copies per title, 10-15 titles per year. Strong backlist of Thich Nhat Hanh titles ensures ongoing reprint demand. Email extrapolated from typical press format.

leads.name: Vegan Richa (Richa Hingle)
leads.notes: Popular food blogger and cookbook author specializing in Indian-inspired vegan cooking. Has published multiple cookbooks. Email extrapolated from site. Strong social following and direct audience relationship suggests self-publishing is a viable option for future titles.

leads.name: Beehive Books (Josh O'Neill)
leads.notes: Specialty literary publisher producing high-quality deluxe editions. Josh O'Neill as publisher. Email extrapolated from domain. Print quality and physical book production are core to their value proposition -- an ideal match for Cushing-Malloy's quality focus.

leads.name: Owl Canyon Press (Robert Merritt)
leads.notes: DEFUNCT -- confirmed inactive as of late 2025. submissions@owlcanyonpress.com SMTP-verified as dead (550, June 30 2026). All other domain addresses (info@, gene@, keith@) also dead. Duotrope removed listing. Original outreach draft in OX should be deleted. Do not include in future runs.

leads.name: Heliotrope Books (Naomi Rosenblatt)
leads.notes: Independent New York-based literary publisher with a thoughtful catalog of fiction and nonfiction. Naomi Rosenblatt as publisher. Email extrapolated from typical small press format. 4-8 titles per year at short print runs.

leads.name: Kate Northrup
leads.notes: Author of Money: A Love Story and Do Less, self-publishing advocate with large direct audience built through courses and community. Maine-based. Email extrapolated from site domain. If pursuing a new book project, direct publishing to her community is a natural path -- and a natural manufacturing need.

leads.name: Lanternfish Press (Amanda Baldeneaux)
leads.notes: Philadelphia-based independent literary press publishing distinctive fiction and memoir. Co-founded by Amanda Baldeneaux and Christine Neulieb. 6-8 titles per year. Email extrapolated from domain. Clear short-run literary manufacturing profile.
